tests.name: init_sh_non_interactive_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh --non-interactive completed without error for sponsored_poc/mobile/swift/full; PR #19 BL-016 confirmed working

tests.name: context7_detection
tests.details: verify-install.sh reports 'Context7 MCP installed' and 'Context7 MCP configured'

tests.name: pre_commit_gate_registered
tests.details: scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh referenced in .claude/settings.json PreToolUse Bash hooks

tests.name: framework_self_guard_active
tests.details: init.sh from $FRAMEWORK exits 1 with 'Refusing to operate inside the Solo Orchestrator framework repo' message (PR #18 confirmed)

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 PHASE1_STEPS (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed via --complete-step without artifact gating

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh exit 0 with 'Phase gates consistent.' but it does not advance the phase

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D confirmed: phase-state.json .current_phase remains 0 after all Phase 1 steps complete and gate check passes; no script auto-advances current_phase

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: After completing all PHASE1_STEPS via --complete-step, run check-phase-gate.sh; it exits 0 but .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase remains 0
framework_bugs.expected: A documented script (check-phase-gate.sh or equivalent) should write the new current_phase value to phase-state.json once all steps are complete and the gate passes
framework_bugs.actual: .current_phase stayed at 0 throughout the entire base run (Phases 1, 2, 3 all completed); no advance command exists

documentation_gaps.gap: No documented command to advance .current_phase between phases; check-phase-gate.sh only validates consistency, does not mutate state

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: All 6 build_loop steps (tests_written, tests_verified_failing, implemented, security_audit, documentation_updated, feature_recorded) succeeded sequentially

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeded after the 5 build-loop steps

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: U-C confirmed: direct shell `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop'` succeeded (commit 5c30cf0). PreToolUse-based gate cannot intercept direct shell git commits.

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1, calling --start-feature uat-feat-2-grace returned OK (PR #17 BL-006 grace window reset confirmed)

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Direct shell commit with .claude/pending-approval.json present succeeded (commit 163902d). Same root cause as U-C — PreToolUse hook does not gate direct shell git invocation; sentinel-aware gating only fires through PreToolUse Bash matcher path.

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: direct shell git commit bypasses BL-006 gate
framework_bugs.reproduction: cd $PROJECT_DIR; git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(x): no loop' (without prior --start-feature/build_loop steps)
framework_bugs.expected: BL-006 gate (or commit-msg hook) should reject feat: commit when no Build Loop is recorded for the feature
framework_bugs.actual: commit succeeded; BL-006 enforcement only fires through PreToolUse Bash matcher (Claude Code agent path), not through direct shell invocation. Same surface as U-C from prior sweep.

tests.name: start_phase3_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase3 returned 0 (with one expected warn 'features since last UAT session')

tests.name: uat_template_lints_clean
tests.details: Linter has no auto-generated populated scenario to lint against. Running lint-uat-scenarios.sh against tests/uat/examples/pre-flight-reference.html exits 1 with 'unreplaced placeholder __BUILD_ID__' — that's expected (it's a template). N/A for this run since no scenario was generated for an actual UAT session.

tests.name: each_uat_step_completes
tests.details: All 9 UAT_STEPS completed sequentially with stub artifacts

documentation_gaps.gap: lint-uat-scenarios.sh requires a 'populated' html input but no script in the project generates one from tests/uat/templates/test-session-template.html; UAT scenario generation pipeline is implicit

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: By design: process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 prints '[FAIL] Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in sponsored poc mode.' and points to upgrade-project.sh --to-production. This is expected guard behavior for sponsored_poc, not a bug. Remaining Phase 4 assertions are N/A for this config.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: N/A — sponsored_poc blocks Phase 4 by design (correct behavior)

tests.name: phase4_completion_state
tests.details: N/A — Phase 4 not entered (sponsored_poc completes at Phase 3 by design)

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: 33 raw matches for password|api[_-]?key|secret|token but all are template/doc references (BITBUCKET_APP_PASSWORD docs example, security-scan-guide example sk-abc123, GITHUB_TOKEN env refs in CI workflow). No literal credentials.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: .claude/process-audit.log not present — N/A; no FORCE/reset events occurred

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD = d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 (matches captured SHA). Untracked .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ are pre-existing, not contamination from this run.

summary: Base sponsored_poc/mobile/full run: init.sh --non-interactive (PR #19) succeeded, all PR #17/#18 fixes (U-E, U-F, U-H, U-N/O, BL-006 grace, fake-remote tolerance, framework-self-guard) confirmed working; reconfirmed U-C (direct shell commits bypass BL-006 and BL-015 gates) and U-D (no script advances current_phase after gate passes) at Medium severity per runbook expectation.
